content.text: I'll start by checking the current state of the SparkPi job and related resources.

input.command:
echo "=== Job ===" && kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o wide 2>&1
echo "=== Pods ===" && kubectl get pods -n spark-pi 2>&1
echo "=== Role ===" && kubectl get role spark-pi-role -n spark-pi -o yaml 2>&1
echo "=== Events ===" && kubectl get events -n spark-pi --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' 2>&1 | tail -30

content.text: Confirmed the issues. Let me find the job YAML file to fix it.

input.command:
find / -path /proc -prune -o -ipath "*spark*" -iname "*.yaml" -print 2>/dev/null | grep -v proc
echo "---"
find / -path /proc -prune -o -ipath "*deploy_spark_pi*" -print 2>/dev/null | grep -v proc

content.text: The referenced file doesn't exist locally — this must be a remote/different cluster context. I'll export the live Job spec directly from the cluster instead.

input.command: kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o yaml

content.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"batch/v1","kind":"Job","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"spark-pi","namespace":"spark-pi"},"spec":{"backoffLimit":0,"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"spark-pi","component":"driver"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/bash","-c","echo \"==============================================\"\necho \"  SparkPi Job\"\necho \"==============================================\"\nexport SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)\n/opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \\\n  --master local[2] \\\n  --driver-memory 512m \\\n  --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \\\n  /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \\\n  1000\nEXIT_CODE=$?\necho \"SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE\"\nexit $EXIT_CODE\n"],"image":"apache/spark:3.5.3-nonexistent","name":"spark-submit","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"1Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"200m","memory":"512"}}}],"restartPolicy":"Never","serviceAccountName":"spark-nonexistent"}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-10T00:01:44Z"
  generation: 1
  labels:
    app: spark-pi
    batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: d775d805-261a-45dd-9195-f6fc9f66e5f4
    batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
    component: driver
    controller-uid: d775d805-261a-45dd-9195-f6fc9f66e5f4
    job-name: spark-pi
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
  resourceVersion: "23198"
  uid: d775d805-261a-45dd-9195-f6fc9f66e5f4
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  completionMode: NonIndexed
  completions: 1
  manualSelector: false
  parallelism: 1
  podReplacementPolicy: TerminatingOrFailed
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: d775d805-261a-45dd-9195-f6fc9f66e5f4
  suspend: false
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: d775d805-261a-45dd-9195-f6fc9f66e5f4
        batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
        component: driver
        controller-uid: d775d805-261a-45dd-9195-f6fc9f66e5f4
        job-name: spark-pi
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3-nonexistent
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: spark-submit
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: "512"
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Never
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: spark-nonexistent
      serviceAccountName: spark-nonexistent
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
status:
  ready: 0
  startTime: "2026-07-10T00:01:44Z"
  terminating: 0
  uncountedTerminatedPods: {}

content.text: I now have the full job spec. I'll write a corrected YAML file, check the service account exists, fix RBAC, delete the old job, and apply the fix.

input.command: echo "=== ServiceAccounts ===" && kubectl get sa -n spark-pi 2>&1

content.content:
=== ServiceAccounts ===
NAME       SECRETS   AGE
default    0         43s
spark-pi   0         42s

tool_use_result.stdout:
=== ServiceAccounts ===
NAME       SECRETS   AGE
default    0         43s
spark-pi   0         42s

content.text: Good, `spark-pi` service account exists. Now let's write the corrected Job YAML.

input.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        component: driver
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: spark-submit
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "200m"
            memory: "512Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "500m"
            memory: "1Gi"
      restartPolicy: Never
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi

content.content: File created successfully at: /workspace/spark-pi-job.yaml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text: Now let's fix the RBAC role and apply the corrected job.

input.command:
kubectl patch role spark-pi-role -n spark-pi --type=json \
  -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/rules/0/resources/-", "value": "pods"}]'
echo "--- Role after patch ---"
kubectl get role spark-pi-role -n spark-pi -o yaml
echo "--- Delete old job ---"
kubectl delete job spark-pi -n spark-pi
echo "--- Apply fixed job ---"
kubectl apply -f /workspace/spark-pi-job.yaml

echo "--- Apply fixed job ---"

content.text: RBAC patched with "pods" resource and job re-applied. Now let's monitor the job until it completes.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}' 2>/dev/null)
  FAILED=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "$STATUS" == "1" ]; then
    echo "Job succeeded!"
    break
  fi
  if [ "$FAILED" != "" ] && [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Job failed, checking..."
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
done
echo "=== Final Job Status ==="
kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi
echo "=== Pods ==="
kubectl get pods -n spark-pi -l app=spark-pi

description:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}' 2>/dev/null)
  FAILED=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "$STATUS" == "1" ]; then
    echo "Job succeeded!"
    break
  fi
  if [ "$FAILED" != "" ] && [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Job failed, checking..."
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
done
echo "=== Final Job Status ==="
kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi
echo "=== Pods ==="
kubectl get pods -n spark-pi -l app=spark-pi
